english: guage (C. *t'ál-ké'* (thal skad)). 2. Sch.: rough-copy, waste-book; pál-po- (phal po) (Cs. also -mo) čé (che), a host, a troop; mi-rgód (mi rgod) pál-po-čé (phal po che) žig (zhig), a troop or set of monsters (v. rgód-pa (rgod pa) II.); gen. like οἱ πολλοί, the mass of the people, majority, great part or number; pál-čé-ba (phal che ba) id. — pál-čén (phal chen), a philosophers' school, called Mahasaṅghika. — pál-čér (phal cher), manifold, for the most part, ordinarily, also = universally; pál-čér (phal cher) čo-ñés (co nyes) _dégs-so ('degs so), they raised a general lamentation Dzl.
W., broad, wide, e.g. a broad valley; pál-méd (phal med), narrow.
instrum. of p'a (pha), I. by the father; v. also p'as-spún (phas spun), sub spun (spun). II. of the opposite side, of the counter-party, e.g. p'as (phas) rgól-ba (rgol ba).
1. num. figure 44. — 2. W. for p'yi (phyi), p'i-pa (phi pa) for p'yi-pa (phyi pa).
(Urdu فكر, Ar. reflexion) W. *ċaṅ p'i-ker med* (*cang phi ker med*) = čaṅ mi sto (cang mi sto) it is no matter, it makes no difference.
Sch.: 1. earthen-ware pitcher. — 2. cup, cupping-glass. — 3. W: *sgó-piṅ* (*sgo ping*), door-hinge.
v. ཕྱིང་པ་; ཕང་བ་; ཕིང་ཅེས་ v. འཕིང་བ་

I. sbst. 1. the upper part of an ascending valley or ravine; p'u (phu) bar (bar) mdo (mdo) (or mda (mda)), the upper, middle, and lower part of such a valley; p'u-ču (phu chu), mountain-torrent, frq.; p'ur (phur) ma (ma) _gro ('gro), p'u (phu) yá-gir (ya gir) ma (ma) _gro ('gro), Glr., do not go to the upper part of the valley; p'u-lhags (phu lhags), higher situated and colder places or districts, opp. to rgya-šód (rgya shod), lower and milder parts. The not unfrequent phrase: p'u-t'ág (phu thag) rčód-pa (rcod pa) or čód-pa (chod pa) was traced by our Lama to its original meaning: the upper part of the valley is shut up (with snow etc.), which is now used in a general sense; k'rúl-bai (khrul bai) p'u-t'ág (phu thag) čód (chod), Mil., prob. shut out all error, prevent every mistake! p'u-t'ág-čód-lugs-kyi (phu thag chod lugs kyi) čos (chos) šig (shig), Mil. seems to be an instruction for making a decision; ṅa (nga) rgás-pa (rgas pa) daṅ (dang) séms (sems) kyi (kyi) p'u-t'ág (phu thag) čód-pas (chod pas) _gró-ba ('gro ba) mi (mi) yóṅ-bar (yong bar) _dug ('dug), prob.: I being old and my spiritual affairs settled (not calling for further improvement), shall probably not travel any more (to India; but you may do so) Mil. cf. p'ugs (phugs). — p'u-pa (phu pa), the inhabitant of an elevated valley. Fig.: p'u (phu) ryo (ryo) mda (mda) dkrug (dkrug), there is agitation above and below, the higher and the lower faculties of the mind are troubled, excited, Mil. — 2. prop. n. Pu, e.g. a village in Upper Kunawar, missionary station of the Church of the United Brethren. — 3. vulgo the spirit or gaseous element of liquors, causing them to foam, effervesce or explode, cf. dbugs (dbugs); perh. to be referred to no. II. II. interjection and imitative sound: p'u (phu) _débs-pa ('debs pa) Glr., *p'u gyáb-če'* (*phu rgyab ce*) W., to make pooh, to blow, to puff, to inflate; p'u (phu) skoṅ (skong), puff it up (the skin etc.), lit.: fill it with pooh! p'us (phus), with the breath; p'us (phus) _débs-pa ('debs pa) Sch. to blow, howl, cry (?); sna-rtsa-p'u (sna rtsa phu), n. of a disease, Lt.

, also p'u-tuṅ (phu tung) Glr., p'u-ruṅ (phu rung) Cs., sleeve; *p'u-rdzús* (*phu rdzus*) C. (false sleeves), p'u-duṅ- (phu dung) (or -tuṅ- (tung)) rtse (rtse) (sleeve-edges) hand-ruffles; mittens, cuffs (to keep the wrist warm).
honour, respect, esteem; p'u-dúd-du (phu dud du) byéd-pa (byed pa), Glr., p'úd-du (phud du) k'úr-ba (khur ba), S.g., to show honour, respect.
, pf. of _búd-pa ('bud pa), to blow, col. used for the latter.

(Sch. also p'un (phun)), a man's elder brother: p'u-nú (phu nu), the elder and the younger, i.e. the two brothers; also the elder and the younger sons (for examples refer to tsan-dán (tsan dan)); in the passage of Dzl 20S, 14, nu (nu) ought to be canceled, and p'u-nú-mo (phu nu mo), VS, 6. 9. should be translated by sister-in-law. p'u-grás (phu gras), Sch., the elder brothers, dub.
